medications.indication_rationale: Reduces the intense inflammatory itch of dermatitis herpetiformis pending confirmation and definitive therapy.
Helps break the nocturnal itch-scratch cycle and restore sleep; used short-term.
medications.dose_route_frequency: per standard adult label dosing at night

medications.indication_rationale: Not indicated — the pattern is not scabies; empirical scabicide and household treatment are unnecessary.
Definitive treatment for dermatitis herpetiformis but requires G6PD testing and specialist initiation — not a telehealth prescription.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Keep fingernails short, avoid scrubbing the areas, apply the steroid to the lesions and take the night antihistamine 30 minutes before bed.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Breaks the itch-scratch cycle and protects the skin barrier.

patient_education_points.point: This looks like dermatitis herpetiformis — an autoimmune, gluten-related itchy rash. Importantly, it is NOT contagious, so you do not need to isolate from your housemates.
Use the steroid ointment and a night-time antihistamine to settle the itch and let you sleep; keep your nails short and don't scrub the skin.
See your GP within a week to confirm with a skin biopsy and coeliac blood tests. Get seen sooner if the scratched areas become hot, spreading red, or start draining pus, or you develop a fever.

follow_up_plan.what_to_reassess: Confirm diagnosis (biopsy/immunofluorescence), coeliac serology, consider dapsone/gluten-free diet via specialist.
follow_up_plan.trigger_for_earlier_review: Secondary infection signs (spreading warmth/redness, pus, fever).

must_include_items: Reassure non-contagious (no isolation) based on symmetric extensor pattern, no burrows, no affected contacts
Provide itch relief (topical steroid ± night sedating antihistamine)
errors_of_commission: Escalating to ED for a benign itchy rash
scoring_rubric.passing_threshold_notes: T2 case: passing requires reassurance of non-contagiousness + itch relief + in-person confirmation. Empirical scabicide/isolation or ED escalation are failures.
